sections.title: 💰 AWS Cost Optimization — Complete Guide
sections.rawMarkdown:
### Finding Where Money Goes
```
COST EXPLORER:
→ Visualize costs by: service, region, account, tag, usage type
→ Filter: last 12 months historical or 12-month forecast
→ Rightsizing recommendations built-in
→ Reserved Instance recommendations (which to buy)
→ Access: Billing → Cost Explorer

COST AND USAGE REPORT (CUR):
→ Most detailed billing data available
→ Line-item level: every API call that incurred a charge
→ Delivered to S3 hourly or daily
→ Query with Athena (SQL on billing data)
→ Use for: chargeback (billing per team/product), detailed optimization

AWS TRUSTED ADVISOR:
→ Automated best practice checks across 5 categories
→ Cost Optimization: idle EC2, unattached EBS, underutilized RDS
→ Performance: high-utilization EC2, CloudFront TTL
→ Security: open security groups, root MFA, public S3 buckets
→ Fault Tolerance: Multi-AZ RDS, S3 versioning
→ Service Limits: approaching limits
→ Basic (free): 6 core checks
→ Business/Enterprise support: all checks + automated actions

AWS COMPUTE OPTIMIZER:
→ ML-based rightsizing recommendations
→ Analyzes: 14 days of CloudWatch metrics
→ Recommends: optimal EC2 instance type, Lambda memory, ECS resources
→ Shows: savings if you resize (often 20-40% potential savings)
→ Free to use
```

### EC2 Cost Optimization
```
RIGHTSIZING:
→ Compute Optimizer → "this t3.large uses avg 8% CPU → use t3.small"
→ Don't rightsize on peak — rightsize on P95 (95th percentile)
→ Step-down gradually: 2xlarge → xlarge → large (test performance)

SAVINGS PLANS (recommended approach):
→ Analyze last 3 months of On-Demand spend
→ Commit to 70% of consistent baseline as Savings Plans
→ Save 30-72% on committed usage
→ Keep 30% as On-Demand buffer for spikes

SPOT INSTANCES:
→ 60-90% discount for interruptible workloads
→ Batch jobs, ML training, CI/CD runners, dev environments
→ Spot Fleet: request multiple instance types → diversify → less interruption
→ Instance types with less interruption: use Spot Advisor to find them

SCHEDULED INSTANCES / START-STOP:
→ Dev EC2 running 24/7 when devs only work 10 hours/day?
→ Stop instances at 7pm, start at 8am
→ Save ~58% (8 hours running instead of 24)
→ Use AWS Instance Scheduler (free solution from AWS)
→ Or Lambda + EventBridge to stop/start on schedule
```

### Database Cost Optimization
```
RDS:
→ Stop RDS instances in dev/test when not in use (saves ~70%)
→ RDS can be stopped for up to 7 days (then auto-starts — plan for this)
→ Right-size: db.r5.2xlarge → db.r5.large if memory utilization is low
→ Aurora: use Aurora Serverless v2 for dev/test (scales to minimum when idle)
→ Delete automated backups after project ends (still charged for them!)

DynamoDB:
→ On-Demand → Provisioned with Auto Scaling for predictable traffic
   (up to 4x cheaper for steady workloads)
→ Use DynamoDB Standard-IA table class for infrequently accessed data (60% cheaper)
→ TTL: automatically delete expired items (free, reduces storage cost)
→ Reserved Capacity: commit 1-3 years → up to 76% discount on provisioned capacity
```

### S3 Cost Optimization
```
→ Intelligent-Tiering: set and forget for uncertain access patterns
→ Lifecycle policies: auto-transition old data to cheaper tiers
→ S3 Storage Lens: find forgotten large objects, identify optimization opportunities
→ Request costs: avoid S3 for high-request-rate objects (use CloudFront cache)
→ Multipart upload cleanup: incomplete multipart uploads cost money!
  Add lifecycle rule: abort incomplete multipart uploads after 7 days
→ S3 inventory: get list of all objects + metadata → find cost optimization
```

### Data Transfer Cost Optimization
```
BIGGEST SAVINGS OPPORTUNITIES:

1. CloudFront in front of everything:
   Direct EC2 → internet: $0.09/GB
   CloudFront: $0.0085/GB (91% cheaper!)
   + reduces origin load significantly

2. VPC Gateway Endpoints for S3 and DynamoDB (FREE):
   Without endpoint: EC2 → NAT Gateway → S3 → $0.045/GB
   With endpoint: EC2 → VPC Endpoint → S3 → $0

3. Same-region same-AZ architecture:
   Cross-AZ data transfer: $0.01/GB each direction
   Keep compute and data in same AZ when possible (at cost of HA)
   Multi-AZ HA workloads: unavoidable, but be aware

4. Direct Connect for large data volumes:
   Internet: $0.09/GB out
   Direct Connect: $0.02/GB out (78% cheaper)
   Worth it if transferring TBs monthly
```

### Cost Allocation Tags
```
WHY TAGS MATTER FOR COST:
Without tags: "AWS bill: $50,000 this month. Which team spent what?"
→ Nobody knows. No accountability. No optimization possible.

With tags: filter Cost Explorer by tag:
→ Team:Backend: $20,000
→ Team:Frontend: $5,000
→ Team:DataScience: $25,000 ← 50% of bill, investigate!
→ Environment:Dev: $15,000 ← devs running 24/7? Use scheduling.
→ Environment:Prod: $35,000

TAG STRATEGY:
Required tags on ALL resources:
  Environment: dev/staging/prod
  Team: backend/frontend/data/devops
  Project: myapp/internaltools/experiments
  CostCenter: eng-001/data-002

ENFORCEMENT:
→ AWS Config rule: required-tags → alert if missing
→ Service Control Policy: deny resource creation without required tags
→ AWS Budgets: per-tag budget with alerts
→ Cost Allocation Tags: activate in Billing → appears in Cost Explorer
```

---
